x-enumeration-literals.name: Fixed Fee
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A pre-agreed flat amount paid to the underwriter regardless of the total issue size or proceeds.

Comment:
Example:
AlphaBank charges a fixed fee of €500,000 for managing a €100M bond issuance, irrespective of the number of bonds sold.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Percentage-Based Fee
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Calculated as a percentage of the total underwritten amount, incentivizing the underwriter to maximize subscription and manage risk efficiently.

Comment:
Example:
For a €500M bond issuance, AlphaBank charges 0.5% as the underwriting fee → €2,500,000.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Tiered Fee
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The fee varies based on thresholds of the underwritten amount. Larger deals may pay lower percentages for higher amounts.

Comment:
Example:

First €100M: 0.6% fee

Next €200M: 0.5% fee

Above €300M: 0.4% fee

x-enumeration-literals.name: Success-Based Fee
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The fee is paid only if the issuance is successfully subscribed or certain milestones are met.

Comment:
Example:
BetaBank charges 0.5% of underwritten bonds only if the minimum subscription of €400M is achieved.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Embedded Fee
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The fee is included in the difference between the issue price and the offer price (the spread/margin). The underwriter earns indirectly through the profit from resale.

Comment:
Example:
Issue price: €99.50, Offer price: €100 → €0.50 per bond is earned as fee/profit by the underwriter.
